title: dispatch_peer_retract emits MessageRetracted even when store soft_delete_message fails
description: In daemon/src/relay.rs dispatch_peer_retract(): if store.soft_delete_message() returns Err, the function returns early without emitting MessageRetracted. However if there is no store (state.store() is None), dispatch_peer_retract emits MessageRetracted without any DB write. More critically: if the store IS present but soft_delete_message fails, the event is NOT emitted — this is the correct case (DB and SSE stay consistent). But the inverse — store absent → event emitted without persistence — means SSE clients see a retraction that is not persisted. On SSE reconnect the message reappears in the store (it was never deleted) while the client believed it was retracted. Trigger: daemon started without --db (store=None), peer sends PeerRetract — client receives MessageRetracted but the message persists in any other view.
